scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, researched against the live API documentation page (app.waiteraid.com/api-docs/index.html) and cross-checked against a third-party historical Apiary mirror (waiterapi.docs.apiary.io). MAJOR CORRECTION vs. the prior wave2 version: the real, currently-documented WaiterAid API has NO REST-shaped resource surface at all -- it is entirely a flat, POST-method, query-string-parameterized method-call API rooted at /wa-api/<methodName> (e.g. /wa-api/searchBooking, /wa-api/addBooking), authenticated via an auth_hash QUERY parameter (obtained once via /wa-api/auth?username=&password=&restaurant=, or configured directly as this bundle's auth_hash secret) plus a mandatory restid query parameter on every call. The prior version's /reservations, /tables, /guests REST-shaped paths do not exist on the real API at all. Every one of the 19 documented real methods is enumerated below; covered ones map to this bundle's 3 streams (reservations, meals, queue) and 6 write actions (add_booking, set_booking_status, edit_booking, add_guest, add_to_queue, delete_from_queue).

excluded.reason: one-time username/password-to-auth_hash exchange; this connector's spec.json expects a pre-obtained auth_hash (matching legacy's own config contract), not an interactive credential-exchange flow -- out of scope for a declarative bundle

lists restaurants accessible to the authenticated user/auth_hash; an account-discovery/administration helper, not restaurant operational data this connector's identity (a single already-configured restid) targets
single-booking detail by bookingId; identical record shape to the reservations stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS: the documented example response is a top-level object with numeric-string keys sitting alongside a sibling 'status' key (`{"status":"OK", 0: {...}, 1: {...}}`), not a named array or the records.keyed_object shape (whose VALUES must all be objects at one dotted path -- 'status' is a string, not an object, so keyed_object would mis-explode it too). Rather than guess a shape that could silently mis-parse or drop the leading/trailing entries, this is excluded pending a live API smoke test to confirm the real wire shape unambiguously
weekly meal-availability-status snapshot (blocked days/times, fully-booked flags per meal); a scheduling-configuration read, not reservation/guest data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
enables/disables a meal slot for a given weekday or date; a scheduling-configuration mutation, not reservation/guest data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
marks a meal as fully-booked for a given date; a scheduling-configuration mutation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
computes a default seating-length duration for a hypothetical booking (amount/meal/date/time), not a stored/syncable record
toggles whether guests may choose their own seating length in the booking widget; a scheduling-configuration mutation, not reservation/guest data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
lists guest-messaging thread ids; messaging is a distinct product surface from reservations/guests/queue, out of scope for this pass
lists messages within a thread; same messaging-surface scoping as getMessageThreads
sends a message to a guest; same messaging-surface scoping as getMessageThreads
